description: During the 2008 global oil price spike, Qantas introduced aggressive fuel surcharges on international flights. While necessary for operational viability, the lack of transparency regarding how these fees correlated with fluctuating oil prices led to significant consumer and regulatory backlash in the Australian market.

key_events.description: ACCC begins investigating price gouging claims regarding airline fuel surcharges.
Widespread media coverage of 'hidden' costs leads to public protest.
Qantas partially adjusts surcharge structure to appease regulators.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling how external economic pressures (oil prices) interact with consumer perception of corporate fairness and regulatory oversight.
